scoreChanges.headline: First full baseline. Three convergent 2026 legal findings establish the design-features-as-harm theory across three jurisdictions: Massachusetts SJC holds Section 230 does not shield Instagram design-features lawsuit (Apr 10, in-window anchor); New Mexico $375M civil penalty (Mar 24); Los Angeles $3M negligence verdict with Meta 70% responsible (Mar 25). Band change Functional → Developing.
evidence: Massachusetts SJC ruling Commonwealth v. Meta Platforms (SJC-13747, April 10, 2026): Section 230 does not shield Meta from Instagram design-features lawsuit. Court held Instagram 'capitalizes on the developmental vulnerabilities of children' and Meta was 'affirmatively misleading consumers about the safety of the Instagram platform.' Primary in-window anchor for ACC, EQU, and INT downgrades.

New Mexico jury verdict (March 24, 2026): $375M civil penalties for consumer protection violations tied to child safety. Meta violated state consumer protection law by misleading users about platform safety. Corroborating prior within assessor evidence window.
Los Angeles jury verdict (March 25, 2026): Meta and YouTube found negligent in social-media addiction case, $3M award with Meta 70% responsible. Corroborating prior within evidence window.
First full reassessment of Meta AI as a distinct ai-labs entry. Published 40.6 Functional pre-dates the 2026 verdict cluster entirely; all three findings postdate the published score.

scoreChanges.headline: OpenAI backs Illinois SB 3444 — liability shield for mass-casualty AI events including CBRN scenarios — in direct opposition to Anthropic's SB 3261 support. ACC/SYS/INT regression. Musk v. OpenAI fraud trial begins April 27, Oakland. Confidence upgrades to high if SB 3444 advances April 24 committee with OpenAI backing.
evidence: Illinois SB 3444 backing (coverage April 10–17, 2026): OpenAI actively supports liability shield exempting AI firms from accountability for mass-casualty events (>100 deaths, >$1B damage), including CBRN weapons scenarios. Experts describe as 'markedly weak approach to corporate liability.' Direct ACC/AB3 and SYS/S4 regression vs Anthropic's opposing SB 3261 stance.

Musk v. OpenAI fraud trial begins April 27, 2026 in Oakland federal court: fraud allegations re: nonprofit-to-for-profit conversion; Altman removal sought. Trial record itself weakens INT/I4 values-alignment dimension regardless of outcome.
Perplexity class-action (April 1, 2026): alleges OpenAI/others shared user chat data with Google and Meta without user consent — B5 consent dimension concern.
No new reparative action, transparency disclosure, or consent/safety structural change found in 14-day window.

confirmations.headline: HOLD resolved. Q1 2026 earnings: EPS $7.23 (beat $6.57 consensus), MCR 83.9% favorable vs 84.8% prior, full-year guidance raised to >$18.25/share. DOJ criminal Medicare Advantage probe acknowledged in risk factors with no new disclosure on criminal charges or cooperation scope. Net +1.9 below threshold.

SCOTUS April 17 (8-0) removes Louisiana coastal damage case to federal court, reducing financial exposure on $745M verdict. Zero ACC change — strategy is legal forum removal, not remediation. Directional negative within threshold.
Parallel SCOTUS beneficiary as second non-settler in Louisiana coastal litigation. No entity-specific compassion event in 14-day window. Directional negative within threshold.

SB 3261 support (mandatory AI safety + child protection liability, opposing OpenAI's SB 3444) is a positive ACC signal offset by dual-track DOD litigation: DC Circuit denied emergency block April 8, but SF federal court preliminary injunction holds. Net neutral from prior downgrade baseline of 63.4.

NAACP + Earthjustice + SELC Clean Air Act lawsuit (April 14) for 27 unpermitted natural gas turbines in majority-Black South Memphis community — 1,700 tons/year NOx potential. Musk response dismissive (cited 'Colossus 2' build schedule; zero acknowledgment of harm). Composite at Critical floor confirmed.

April 21 WSWS coverage confirms 1,000+ layoffs and full elimination of Marvel Visual Development team. Unite Here union: 'AI should not be used as an excuse to eliminate workers.' Yesterday's −8.6 (Established → Functional) already applied; no additional delta today.

PDX9 Troutdale worker death (Apr 6) and NLRB JFK8 first-ever bargaining order (Apr 2) remain the governing signals; both already priced in. Oregon OSHA reclassification decision (work-related vs. non-work-related) is the pending binary trigger for a downgrade proposal.

90,000+ active talc lawsuits (up from 66,000 assessed at prior scan); third bankruptcy attempt denied; $7B settlement offer withdrawn. $50M mesothelioma verdict March 2026. No April-window-specific event drives a threshold-crossing delta.

Year-4 war anniversary (April 15). UN 2026 humanitarian plan funded at 16% of $9B required. 34M (65% of population) in urgent need. Two active famines (El Fasher, Kadugli). UN relief chief: 'World failing Sudan.' Critical floor confirmed.

UNMISS mandate expires April 30 — UNSC vote imminent. Abiemnom massacre March 1: up to 178 killed including ~90 civilians — worst mass casualty event since 2018. Kiir-Machar peace deal abandoned; civil war risk highest since 2018. Critical floor confirmed; UNMISS expiry is standing downgrade trigger if mandate lapses.

Post-war crackdown intensifying: Nasrin Sotoudeh (prominent human rights lawyer) arrested April 1–2 without warrant, transferred to undisclosed location. 13+ political executions since March 19 including PMOI members and protesters. Judiciary ordering abnormal-speed reviews outside standard frameworks. Directional downgrade signal within threshold.

Global Sumud Flotilla departed April 15 from Barcelona (70 vessels, ~1,000 volunteers, Greenpeace Arctic Sunrise). 2,400+ documented ceasefire violations since October 10 ceasefire. Only 37% of allocated aid trucks entered Gaza through Feb 14. All border crossings closed February 28. 37 international NGOs deregistered. Directional downgrade within threshold; flotilla interception would trigger proposal.

April 18 Montreux ceasefire (Qatar-mediated): mutual commitment to protect civilians, release 477 detainees. Positive ACT/BND signal immediately undermined by same-day drone strike killing 4 civilians (mostly women and children) in South Kivu. M23 demands blanket war-crimes immunity; DRC resists. Net small positive on structure creation; fragility limits lift.

NYC Comptroller Feb 5 letter (license-plate data / ICE enforcement) is pre-window (before April 7 cutoff). Under 14-day recency rule: confirmation only. Assessed −5.0 at exact threshold boundary; low confidence given pre-window anchor. May proxy-cycle shareholder action would generate a proposal.
OCC trade-surveillance consent order terminated April 16 (positive ACC signal) offset by ERISA prescription drug cost class-action partial MTD survival March 9 (negative ACC signal). Net near-zero.

No in-window material event. Decentralized subsidiary model and board opposition to human-capital reporting remain durable structural limits. May 2 annual meeting watch item for shareholder human-capital vote.

sectorAlerts.alert: Illinois SB 3261 (Anthropic-backed: mandatory AI safety plans, child harm liability) and SB 3444 (OpenAI-backed: mass-casualty liability shield including CBRN scenarios) face committee hearing April 24. The two bills represent directly opposing governance philosophies from the two top-ranked AI labs. First state legislature to bifurcate major labs on liability. Outcome material for ACC, SYS, and INT dimensions across the ai-labs index.

Elon Musk v. OpenAI fraud trial begins April 27, Oakland federal court. Jury selection starts that day. $500B+ structural implications. Fraud allegations cover nonprofit-to-for-profit conversion and Altman's role. If Musk prevails, structural unwind of for-profit conversion could be ordered. Implicates OpenAI INT/I5 and SYS dimensions. Trial record alone is a sustained ACC/INT stress test.

SCOTUS April 17 (8-0) in Chevron USA v. Plaquemines Parish: Louisiana coastal damage cases removed to federal court under Federal Officer Removal Statute. Jeopardizes $745M state jury verdict. Chevron and ExxonMobil are the only two of the original defendants that did not settle. Ruling sets precedent for up to 40 similar state suits, offering oil industry structurally easier path to federal courts.

Three 2026 rulings establish that platform design features fall outside Section 230 protection: Massachusetts SJC (April 10, Instagram design-features), New Mexico $375M civil penalty (March 24), Los Angeles negligence verdict (March 25). The design-features-as-harm theory is now validated by a state supreme court. Portable to any platform using algorithmic amplification, autoplay, or notification-based engagement architecture. Affects Meta AI (downgrade proposal tonight), and creates sector-wide downward prior for YouTube, TikTok, and Snapchat.

Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer resigned April 20 under OIG investigation (hostile work environment, inappropriate conduct with bodyguard, drinking on job, misuse of travel). Acting Secretary Keith Sonderling lacks Senate-confirmed authority for major enforcement actions. Structural gap in DOL enforcement posture during active Amazon (NLRB bargaining order), Walt Disney (Unite Here contract), and Home Depot (ICE data) labor scrutiny. Apply as downward SYS/S5 prior in all Fortune 500 labor-practice rotation assessments until Senate-confirmed Secretary in place.

NAACP + Earthjustice + SELC filed Clean Air Act lawsuit April 14 against xAI for operating 27 unpermitted natural gas turbines at Southaven, MS data center site. Potential 1,700 tons/year NOx, 180 tons/year fine particulate in majority-Black community already failing smog standards. Musk response dismissive — cited build schedule, no acknowledgment of harm. First AI infrastructure environmental justice case in US litigation history. Sets precedent category for AI-lab infrastructure assessments across the index.

Five simultaneous tier-1 humanitarian emergencies active: (1) Sudan year-4 war — 34M in urgent need, 16% of $9B UN plan funded; (2) South Sudan UNMISS mandate expires April 30 — civil war risk highest since 2018; (3) Iran post-war crackdown — political executions, Sotoudeh enforced disappearance; (4) Israel-Gaza — flotilla at sea, aid blockade persists despite ceasefire; (5) DRC — ceasefire signed April 18, immediately violated by drone strike. UN emergency mechanisms active across all five.

Third consecutive night of large preliminary deltas on F500 high-scorers with no in-window anchor: Merck (rank 13, published 87.7, assessed 76.8, Δ −10.9) and P&G (rank 14, published 86.1, assessed 71.6, Δ −14.5). Dimension profiles resemble uniform-4.5 artifact corrected in Batch 5 (State Street, Abbott, Microsoft, Nucor, Ecolab on April 20). No proposals generated under 14-day recency rule — no in-window event anchors the delta. Pattern now spans 7 entities across 3 nights. Founder should consider proactive methodology note before external discovery.

insights: Meta AI's first full baseline is anchored by a state supreme court ruling, not a preliminary estimate. Three independent 2026 legal findings have established the design-features-as-harm theory across three jurisdictions within a 17-day window. The Massachusetts SJC decision in particular — a state supreme court holding that Section 230 does not shield Instagram design-feature liability — is durable, precedent-setting, and in-window. This is the strongest evidentiary foundation for any first baseline in the pipeline's history. The −11.2 delta and band change to Developing reflect a real legal record, not an assessor disagreement with the prior estimate.

The AI governance bifurcation between Anthropic and OpenAI is no longer a matter of values statements — it is being written into competing state statutes. The April 24 Illinois committee vote on SB 3261 (Anthropic: child safety liability) vs. SB 3444 (OpenAI: mass-casualty liability shield) is the most concrete policy divergence between two scored entities in the same index that this benchmark has ever documented. Both labs are active legislative advocates in the same session, on opposite sides. The benchmark can now say, with scored entities and documented evidence, that these two organizations have made structurally different governance choices in 2026.

The F500 Generation-2 overscoring signal is now large enough to require a founder decision on methodology communication. After three consecutive nights — April 19 (GEO Group, CoreCivic baseline corrections), April 20 (Batch 5: State Street, Abbott, Microsoft, Nucor, Ecolab), and April 21 (Merck −10.9 flagged, P&G −14.5 flagged) — the pattern of published F500 high-scorers scoring materially lower under evidence-based assessment is visible across at minimum 7 entities. A proactive methodology note is more consistent with the benchmark's independence policy than waiting for each entity's in-window event to produce the proposals piecemeal. The window for a proactive explanation is still open.

The Accountability (ACC) dimension remains the universal floor across all seven nights and all four indexes. Every sector, every night: the entities that are moving downward are doing so because they deny harm, fight accountability in court, or comply only under legal compulsion. Entities that receive positive signals do so because an external body (a jury, a regulator, a union) forced a structural change. There are no entities in the pipeline generating voluntary upward ACC movement. This is not a calibration finding — it is the benchmark's most durable cross-sectoral result and the central claim it should be building its public analytical voice around.

sectorTrends.trend: Governance bifurcation. Anthropic (63.4 Established) and OpenAI (31.3 Developing proposed) are taking directly opposing positions in Illinois state legislation this week, with a committee vote April 24. The ai-labs index now spans Established through Critical in a single sector, with the ACC and INT dimensions the primary differentiators at every tier.

SCOTUS Chevron removal ruling (April 17) structurally reduces state-level accountability exposure for oil companies on coastal damage litigation. Both Chevron and Exxon Mobil confirmed at directional negative (Δ −3.5) within threshold. Zero ACC change — legal strategy is forum removal, not remediation. Sets precedent for up to 40 similar state suits.

Five simultaneous tier-1 crises. Sudan (0.0) and South Sudan (0.0) at floor with UNMISS expiry April 30 as imminent structural risk. Iran and Israel both show directional downgrade signals below threshold. DRC shows fragile net positive from Montreux ceasefire mechanism. The humanitarian cluster is deepening, not stabilizing.

Section 230 erosion accelerating in 2026 case law. Three verdicts in 17 days establish design-features-as-harm theory. Meta AI drops to Developing. Sector-wide downward prior now applicable to any platform using algorithmic engagement architecture. Full scope not yet priced into Fortune 500 social-media rotation assessments.
